architecture_summary: Segmenter is a fully transformer-based encoder-decoder for semantic segmentation built on ViT patch embeddings. It uses a transformer encoder over image patches and either a point-wise linear decoder or a mask transformer decoder to produce pixel-level class annotations without convolutions.

novel_contributions: We propose a novel approach to semantic segmentation based on the Vision Transformer (ViT) that does not use convolutions, captures contextual information by design and outperforms FCN based approaches.

We present a family of models with varying levels of resolution which allows to trade-off between precision and runtime, ranging from state-of-the-art performance to models with fast inference and good performances.
We propose a transformer-based decoder generating class masks which outperforms our linear baseline and can be extended to perform more general image segmentation tasks.
We demonstrate that our approach yields state-of-the-art results on both ADE20K and Pascal Context datasets and is competitive on Cityscapes.

key_claims.claim: Segmenter is a pure transformer encoder-decoder for semantic segmentation that models global image context at every stage and does not use convolutions.
A point-wise linear decoder already gives excellent results, and a mask transformer decoder further improves performance.
Large models with small patch sizes perform better, and the best model reaches 53.63% mIoU on ADE20K.
Segmenter outperforms the state of the art on both ADE20K and Pascal Context and is competitive on Cityscapes.
Stochastic depth improves transformer training for segmentation, while dropout hurts performance.
Reducing patch size is a key factor for segmentation performance and improves boundary quality.
The mask transformer consistently improves over the linear decoder.
ImageNet pre-training is important; Seg-S/16 with ImageNet-21k pre-training and SGD reaches 45.37% mIoU on ADE20K validation set.
